title: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy
chief_complaint: My son walks on his toes, falls frequently, and can't run or jump like other kids his age. He uses his hands to push on his thighs to stand up. He seems to be getting weaker, not stronger. My brother had similar problems and died at 18.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Gowers sign positive (uses hands to 'walk up' his thighs to stand from floor - classic for proximal muscle weakness). Waddling (Trendelenburg) gait. Toe walking.
physical_exam.Musculoskeletal: Pseudohypertrophy of calves (fatty/fibrous replacement of muscle - NOT true hypertrophy). Proximal muscle weakness (shoulder girdle, hip flexors) 4/5. Distal strength 5/5.
physical_exam.Reflexes: Knee jerks absent. Ankle jerks present.
physical_exam.Cognitive: Mild speech delay (30% have cognitive involvement)

hidden_data.ck: CK 25,000 U/L (massively elevated - normal <200). Muscle membrane breakdown.
hidden_data.genetic: DMD gene deletion exons 45-52 (out-of-frame deletion). Confirms Duchenne (out-of-frame = no dystrophin produced).
hidden_data.muscle_biopsy: Complete absence of dystrophin on immunohistochemistry. Fatty replacement of muscle.
hidden_data.echo: Normal cardiac function (LV EF 60%). Will need regular surveillance.
hidden_data.ecg: Sinus tachycardia. Tall R waves in V1-V2 (RV predominance - normal in children).

correct_diagnosis: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD) - Out-of-Frame DMD Gene Deletion
key_learning_points: DMD = X-linked recessive progressive muscular dystrophy. Most common muscular dystrophy of childhood. Incidence 1:3500 male births

Pathophysiology: Dystrophin deficiency → muscle membrane fragility → necrosis → fibrosis/fatty replacement. Out-of-frame DMD mutation = NO dystrophin (Duchenne). In-frame = partial dystrophin (Becker - milder, later onset)
Classic signs: Proximal weakness (pelvic > shoulder girdle), Gowers maneuver (pathognomonic), pseudohypertrophy of calves, waddling gait, toe walking. Onset 3-5 years. Loss of ambulation 8-12 years

CK massively elevated (10-50× normal). Genetic testing confirms. Muscle biopsy if genetics negative. Cardiac involvement: Dilated cardiomyopathy (100% by age 18, leading cause of death). Respiratory failure in late teens (requires NIPPV)

Treatment: CORTICOSTEROIDS (Prednisone 0.75mg/kg/day or Deflazacort 0.9mg/kg/day) - prolong ambulation by 2-3 years, preserve respiratory function, delay cardiomyopathy. Exon-skipping therapies (Eteplirsen, Golodirsen) for specific mutations. Gene therapy (Delandistrogene moxeparvovec) recently approved. Multidisciplinary care: PT/OT, cardiac (ACEi at age 10), respiratory (NIPPV), orthopedic (scoliosis)

feedback.attending_note: Start Prednisone 0.75mg/kg/day immediately. Physical therapy to maintain ambulation. Cardiac baseline echo and annual surveillance (start ACEi age 10). Genetic counseling - mother is obligate carrier. Family planning and carrier testing for female relatives. Connect with MDA clinic and support groups. Discuss clinical trial options.
